info.title: Explore RPC and user architecture (@explore subagent)

parts.text:
I need to understand the existing RPC architecture and user-related code in this project. Please explore thoroughly and return:

1. The full contents of `src/rpc/router/index.ts` - the main RPC router
2. The full contents of `src/rpc/client.ts` - the RPC client
3. The full contents of `src/rpc/middleware.ts` - the procedure chain
4. The full contents of `src/rpc/context.ts` - the RPC context
5. Find and show any existing RPC route files that implement list endpoints (like client.list) - check `src/rpc/router/` for all files
6. The user-related schema in `src/schema/` - find and show the user table schema
7. The access control file `src/lib/auth/access-control.ts` - show the resources, actions, and permission definitions
8. The auth server file `src/lib/auth/server.ts` - to understand user model
9. Any existing user service in `src/services/`
10. Check if there's already a `src/rpc/router/users.ts` file

Return ALL file contents in full - do not truncate or summarize. This is critical for planning.
The user wants a thorough exploration of the RPC architecture and user-related code. Let me make multiple parallel calls to gather all the information requested.
I'll systematically explore all the requested files. Let me start by reading the known files and searching for patterns in parallel.

1: import { createORPCClient } from "@orpc/client";
2: import { RPCLink } from "@orpc/client/fetch";
3: import type { RouterClient } from "@orpc/server";
4: import { createRouterClient } from "@orpc/server";
5: import { createTanstackQueryUtils } from "@orpc/tanstack-query";
6: import { createIsomorphicFn } from "@tanstack/react-start";
7: import { getRequestHeaders } from "@tanstack/react-start/server";
8: 
9: import router from "@/rpc/router";
10: 
11: const getORPCClient = createIsomorphicFn()
12:   .server(() =>
13:     createRouterClient(router, {
14:       context: () => ({
15:         headers: getRequestHeaders(),
16:       }),
17:     }),
18:   )
19:   .client((): RouterClient<typeof router> => {
20:     const link = new RPCLink({
21:       url: `${window.location.origin}/api/rpc`,
22:     });
23:     return createORPCClient(link);
24:   });
25: 
26: export const client: RouterClient<typeof router> = getORPCClient();
27: 
28: export const rpc = createTanstackQueryUtils(client);

(End of file - total 28 lines)
</content>

<path>/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/rpc/middleware.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { ORPCError } from "@orpc/server";
2: 
3: import type { Action, Resource } from "@/lib/auth/access-control";
4: import { auth } from "@/lib/auth/server";
5: import type { SubordinateCache } from "@/services/supervisor-hierarchy-service";
6: 
7: import { type AuthContext, base } from "./context";
8: 
9: type PermissionMeta = {
10:   permission?: { resource: Resource; action: Action };
11: };
12: 
13: const baseWithMeta = base.$meta<PermissionMeta>({});
14: 
15: export const authMiddleware = baseWithMeta.middleware(
16:   async ({ context, next }) => {
17:     const sessionData = await auth.api.getSession({
18:       headers: context.headers || {},
19:     });
20: 
21:     if (!sessionData?.session || !sessionData?.user) {
22:       throw new ORPCError("UNAUTHORIZED", {
23:         message: "You must be signed in",
24:       });
25:     }
26: 
27:     const { session, user } = sessionData;
28:     const subordinateCache: SubordinateCache = new Map();
29:     return next({
30:       context: { session, subordinateCache, user } satisfies AuthContext,
31:     });
32:   },
33: );
34: 
35: export const protectedProcedure = baseWithMeta
36:   .use(authMiddleware)
37:   .use(async ({ context, next, procedure }) => {
38:     const required = procedure["~orpc"].meta.permission;
39:     if (!required) return next();
40:     if (
41:       context.user.permissions.includes(
42:         `${required.resource}:${required.action}`,
43:       )
44:     ) {
45:       return next();
46:     }
47: 
48:     throw new ORPCError("FORBIDDEN", {
49:       data: { requiredPermission: required },
50:       message: `Missing permission: ${required.resource}:${required.action}`,
51:     });
52:   });

(End of file - total 52 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: import { createAccessControl } from "better-auth/plugins/access";
2: 
3: import { ROLE_DISPLAY_NAMES } from "../constants";
4: 
5: export const ac = createAccessControl({
6:   client_contracts: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
7:   clients: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
8:   job_mandates: [
9:     "create",
10:     "read",
11:     "update",
12:     "archive",
13:     "assign",
14:     "link_prospect",
15:     "verify",
16:   ],
17:   notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
18:   prospects: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
19:   reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
20:   tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
21:   team_members: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "manage-roles"],
22: });
23: 
24: export const roles = {
25:   admin: ac.newRole({
26:     client_contracts: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
27:     clients: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
28:     job_mandates: [
29:       "create",
30:       "read",
31:       "update",
32:       "archive",
33:       "assign",
34:       "link_prospect",
35:       "verify",
36:     ],
37:     notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
38:     prospects: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
39:     reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
40:     tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
41:     team_members: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "manage-roles"],
42:   }),
43:   bd: ac.newRole({
44:     client_contracts: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
45:     clients: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
46:     job_mandates: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
47:     notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
48:     prospects: ["create", "read"],
49:     reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
50:     tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
51:     team_members: ["read"],
52:   }),
53:   caller: ac.newRole({
54:     job_mandates: ["read"],
55:     notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
56:     prospects: ["create", "read", "update"],
57:     reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
58:     tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
59:     team_members: ["read"],
60:   }),
61:   qc: ac.newRole({
62:     job_mandates: ["read", "verify"],
63:     notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
64:     prospects: ["read"],
65:     reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
66:     tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
67:     team_members: ["read"],
68:   }),
69:   rm: ac.newRole({
70:     client_contracts: ["read"],
71:     clients: ["read"],
72:     job_mandates: ["read", "assign"],
73:     notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
74:     prospects: ["create", "read"],
75:     reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
76:     tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
77:     team_members: ["read"],
78:   }),
79:   sc: ac.newRole({
80:     job_mandates: ["read", "link_prospect"],
81:     notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
82:     prospects: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
83:     reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
84:     tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
85:     team_members: ["read"],
86:   }),
87:   tl: ac.newRole({
88:     job_mandates: ["read", "assign", "link_prospect"],
89:     notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
90:     prospects: ["create", "read"],
91:     reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
92:     tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],
93:     team_members: ["read"],
94:   }),
95: } as const;
96: 
97: export type RoleCode = keyof typeof roles | "custom";
98: export type Statement = typeof ac.statements;
99: export type Resource = keyof Statement;
100: export type Action<S extends Resource = Resource> = Statement[S][number];
101: export type PermissionSet = Record<string, string[]>;
102: export type ResolvedPermissions = string[];
103: 
104: export function resolvePermissions({
105:   role,
106:   permissions,
107: }: {
108:   role: RoleCode;
109:   permissions?: PermissionSet | null;
110: }): ResolvedPermissions {
111:   if (role === "custom") {
112:     if (!permissions) return [];
113:     return Object.entries(permissions).flatMap(([domain, actions]) =>
114:       actions.map((access) => `${domain}:${access}`),
115:     );
116:   }
117: 
118:   if (!(role in roles)) return [];
119:   const roleDef = roles[role];
120:   if (!roleDef) return [];
121: 
122:   return Object.entries(roleDef.statements).flatMap(([domain, actions]) =>
123:     actions.map((access) => `${domain}:${access}`),
124:   );
125: }
126: 
127: export function listPredefinedRoles() {
128:   const result: {
129:     code: RoleCode;
130:     displayName: string;
131:     permissions: PermissionSet;
132:   }[] = [];
133: 
134:   for (const [code, roleDef] of Object.entries(roles)) {
135:     const permissions: PermissionSet = {};
136:     for (const resource of Object.keys(ac.statements) as Resource[]) {
137:       const allowed = (roleDef as unknown as Record<string, string[]>)[
138:         resource
139:       ];
140:       if (allowed && allowed.length > 0) {
141:         permissions[resource] = allowed;
142:       }
143:     }
144:     result.push({
145:       code: code as RoleCode,
146:       displayName: ROLE_DISPLAY_NAMES[code as RoleCode],
147:       permissions,
148:     });
149:   }
150: 
151:   result.push({
152:     code: "custom",
153:     displayName: ROLE_DISPLAY_NAMES.custom,
154:     permissions: {},
155:   });
156: 
157:   return result;
158: }

(End of file - total 158 lines)
</content>

import { createAccessControl } from "better-auth/plugins/access";

import { ROLE_DISPLAY_NAMES } from "../constants";

export const ac = createAccessControl({
  client_contracts: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
  clients: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
  job_mandates: [
    "create",
    "read",
    "update",
    "archive",
    "assign",
    "link_prospect",
    "verify",
  ],
  notification: ["read", "update", "archive"],
  prospects: ["create", "read", "update", "archive"],
  reminders: ["create", "read", "archive"],
  tasks: ["create", "read", "update", "archive", "assign"],

state.output:
<path>/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/lib/auth/server.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { drizzleAdapter } from "@better-auth/drizzle-adapter";
2: import { type BetterAuthOptions, betterAuth } from "better-auth";
3: import { APIError } from "better-auth/api";
4: import { admin, customSession, phoneNumber } from "better-auth/plugins";
5: import { tanstackStartCookies } from "better-auth/tanstack-start";
6: import { eq } from "drizzle-orm";
7: 
8: import {
9:   ac,
10:   type RoleCode,
11:   resolvePermissions,
12:   roles,
13: } from "@/lib/auth/access-control";
14: import { db } from "@/lib/db/server";
15: import * as schema from "@/schema";
16: 
17: const options = {
18:   database: drizzleAdapter(db, {
19:     provider: "pg",
20:     schema,
21:   }),
22:   databaseHooks: {
23:     user: {
24:       create: {
25:         before: async (_user, ctx) => {
26:           const path = ctx?.path ?? "";
27:           if (path === "/admin/create-user") return;
28:           throw new APIError("BAD_REQUEST", {
29:             message: "Signups are disabled",
30:           });
31:         },
32:       },
33:     },
34:   },
35:   emailAndPassword: {
36:     enabled: false,
37:   },
38:   plugins: [
39:     phoneNumber({
40:       sendOTP: async ({ phoneNumber: phone, code }) => {
41:         const [existingUser] = await db
42:           .select()
43:           .from(schema.user)
44:           .where(eq(schema.user.phoneNumber, phone));
45: 
46:         if (!existingUser) {
47:           throw new APIError("BAD_REQUEST", { message: "No account found" });
48:         }
49:         if (existingUser.banned) {
50:           throw new APIError("FORBIDDEN", { message: "Account is suspended" });
51:         }
52:         console.log(`[OTP] Phone: ${phone} | Code: ${code}`);
53:       },
54:     }),
55:     admin({ ac, roles }),
56:     tanstackStartCookies(),
57:   ],
58:   session: {
59:     expiresIn: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30,
60:   },
61:   user: {
62:     additionalFields: {
63:       permissions: {
64:         defaultValue: null,
65:         input: false,
66:         required: false,
67:         type: "json",
68:       },
69:       supervisorId: {
70:         defaultValue: null,
71:         input: false,
72:         required: false,
73:         type: "string",
74:       },
75:     },
76:   },
77: } satisfies BetterAuthOptions;
78: 
79: export const auth = betterAuth({
80:   ...options,
81:   plugins: [
82:     customSession(async ({ user, session }) => {
83:       const role = (user.role ?? "custom") as RoleCode;
84:       const permissions = resolvePermissions({
85:         permissions: user.permissions,
86:         role,
87:       });
88:       return { session, user: { ...user, permissions, role } };
89:     }, options),
90:     ...(options.plugins ?? []),
91:   ],
92: });

(End of file - total 92 lines)
</content>

Good, I have the main files. Now I need to:
1. Read the client.ts RPC router file (it has a list endpoint)
2. Read the auth.ts schema (user table)
3. Read the schema/index.ts to see how schemas are organized
4. Check if there's a users.ts in the RPC router (it's NOT in the glob results, so it doesn't exist)
5. Check if there's any user service in services/ (NOT in the glob results)

Let me also read the existing list endpoint implementations as examples, and the auth schema.
Now let me read the RPC router files with list endpoints, the auth/user schema, and the schema index:

<path>/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/rpc/router/client.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { getPresignedUploadUrl, getR2PublicUrl } from "@/lib/r2";
2: import { protectedProcedure } from "@/rpc/middleware";
3: import {
4:   archiveClientSchema,
5:   createClientSchema,
6:   getClientByIdSchema,
7:   getUploadUrlSchema,
8:   listClientEventsSchema,
9:   listClientsSchema,
10:   updateClientSchema,
11: } from "@/rpc/schema/client";
12: import { listClientEvents } from "@/services/client-event-service";
13: import {
14:   archiveClient as archiveClientService,
15:   createClient,
16:   getClient,
17:   listClients,
18:   updateClient,
19: } from "@/services/client-service";
20: 
21: export const clientCreate = protectedProcedure
22:   .meta({ permission: { action: "create", resource: "clients" } })
23:   .input(createClientSchema)
24:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
25:     const client = await createClient({
26:       ...input,
27:       createdBy: context.user.id,
28:     });
29:     return client;
30:   });
31: 
32: export const clientUpdate = protectedProcedure
33:   .meta({ permission: { action: "update", resource: "clients" } })
34:   .input(updateClientSchema)
35:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
36:     const { id, ...updates } = input;
37:     const userId = context.user.id;
38:     const client = await updateClient(id, updates, userId);
39:     return client;
40:   });
41: 
42: export const clientArchive = protectedProcedure
43:   .meta({ permission: { action: "archive", resource: "clients" } })
44:   .input(archiveClientSchema)
45:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
46:     const userId = context.user.id;
47:     const client = await archiveClientService(input.id, userId);
48:     return client;
49:   });
50: 
51: export const clientGetById = protectedProcedure
52:   .meta({ permission: { action: "read", resource: "clients" } })
53:   .input(getClientByIdSchema)
54:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
55:     const client = await getClient(input.id);
56:     if (!client) throw new Error("Client not found");
57:     return client;
58:   });
59: 
60: export const clientList = protectedProcedure
61:   .meta({ permission: { action: "read", resource: "clients" } })
62:   .input(listClientsSchema)
63:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
64:     const result = await listClients({
65:       cursor: input.cursor,
66:       filters: {
67:         archived: input.archived,
68:         assigneeId: input.assigneeId,
69:         search: input.search,
70:       },
71:       limit: input.limit,
72:       sortBy: input.sortBy,
73:       sortOrder: input.sortOrder,
74:       subordinateCache: context.subordinateCache,
75:       userId: context.user.id,
76:     });
77:     return result;
78:   });
79: 
80: export const clientListEvents = protectedProcedure
81:   .meta({ permission: { action: "read", resource: "clients" } })
82:   .input(listClientEventsSchema)
83:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
84:     return listClientEvents(input.clientId, input.cursor, input.limit ?? 50);
85:   });
86: 
87: export const clientGetUploadUrl = protectedProcedure
88:   .meta({ permission: { action: "create", resource: "clients" } })
89:   .input(getUploadUrlSchema)
90:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
91:     const key = `clients/${crypto.randomUUID()}/${input.filename}`;
92:     const url = await getPresignedUploadUrl(key, input.contentType);
93:     return { key, publicUrl: getR2PublicUrl(key), url };
94:   });

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { ORPCError } from "@orpc/server";
2: import { hashPassword } from "better-auth/crypto";
3: import { and, eq } from "drizzle-orm";
4: import { alias } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
5: 
6: import { listPredefinedRoles } from "@/lib/auth/access-control";
7: import { db } from "@/lib/db/server";
8: import { protectedProcedure } from "@/rpc/middleware";
9: import {
10:   archiveUserSchema,
11:   createUserSchema,
12:   resetPasswordSchema,
13:   updateUserSchema,
14: } from "@/rpc/schema/admin";
15: import { account, session, user } from "@/schema";
16: import { validateNoCircularSupervisor } from "@/services/supervisor-hierarchy-service";
17: 
18: export const createUser = protectedProcedure
19:   .meta({
20:     permission: {
21:       action: "manage-roles" as const,
22:       resource: "team_members" as const,
23:     },
24:   })
25:   .input(createUserSchema)
26:   .handler(async ({ context, input }) => {
27:     if (input.role === "custom") {
28:       if (!input.permissions || Object.keys(input.permissions).length === 0) {
29:         throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
30:           message: "permissions must be non-empty when role is 'custom'",
31:         });
32:       }
33:     }
34: 
35:     if (input.role !== "admin" && !input.supervisorId) {
36:       throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
37:         message: "Non-admin users must have a supervisor",
38:       });
39:     }
40: 
41:     if (input.role === "admin") {
42:       input.supervisorId = undefined;
43:     }
44: 
45:     const normalizedEmail = input.email.toLowerCase();
46: 
47:     const [existing] = await db
48:       .select({ id: user.id })
49:       .from(user)
50:       .where(eq(user.email, normalizedEmail));
51: 
52:     if (existing) {
53:       throw new ORPCError("CONFLICT", {
54:         message: "A user with this email already exists",
55:       });
56:     }
57: 
58:     const id = crypto.randomUUID();
59: 
60:     if (input.supervisorId) {
61:       const [supervisor] = await db
62:         .select({ id: user.id })
63:         .from(user)
64:         .where(eq(user.id, input.supervisorId));
65: 
66:       if (!supervisor) {
67:         throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
68:           message: "Supervisor not found",
69:         });
70:       }
71: 
72:       await validateNoCircularSupervisor(
73:         id,
74:         input.supervisorId,
75:         context.subordinateCache,
76:       );
77:     }
78: 
79:     await db.insert(user).values({
80:       email: normalizedEmail,
81:       id,
82:       name: input.name,
83:       permissions: input.role === "custom" ? input.permissions : null,
84:       role: input.role,
85:       supervisorId: input.supervisorId ?? null,
86:     });
87: 
88:     const hashedPassword = await hashPassword(input.password);
89:     await db.insert(account).values({
90:       accountId: id,
91:       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
92:       password: hashedPassword,
93:       providerId: "credential",
94:       userId: id,
95:     });
96: 
97:     return { id };
98:   });
99: 
100: export const updateUser = protectedProcedure
101:   .meta({
102:     permission: {
103:       action: "manage-roles" as const,
104:       resource: "team_members" as const,
105:     },
106:   })
107:   .input(updateUserSchema)
108:   .handler(async ({ context, input }) => {
109:     const {
110:       userId,
111:       role,
112:       permissions,
113:       banned,
114:       banReason,
115:       banExpires,
116:       name,
117:       email,
118:       supervisorId,
119:     } = input;
120: 
121:     if (role === "custom") {
122:       if (!permissions || Object.keys(permissions).length === 0) {
123:         throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
124:           message: "permissions must be non-empty when role is 'custom'",
125:         });
126:       }
127:     }
128: 
129:     const [existing] = await db
130:       .select({ role: user.role, supervisorId: user.supervisorId })
131:       .from(user)
132:       .where(eq(user.id, userId));
133: 
134:     if (!existing) {
135:       throw new ORPCError("NOT_FOUND", { message: "User not found" });
136:     }
137: 
138:     const effectiveRole = role ?? existing.role;
139:     const effectiveSupervisorId =
140:       supervisorId !== undefined ? supervisorId : existing.supervisorId;
141: 
142:     if (effectiveRole !== "admin" && !effectiveSupervisorId) {
143:       throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
144:         message: "Non-admin users must have a supervisor",
145:       });
146:     }
147: 
148:     if (supervisorId) {
149:       const [supervisor] = await db
150:         .select({ id: user.id })
151:         .from(user)
152:         .where(eq(user.id, supervisorId));
153: 
154:       if (!supervisor) {
155:         throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
156:           message: "Supervisor not found",
157:         });
158:       }
159: 
160:       await validateNoCircularSupervisor(
161:         userId,
162:         supervisorId,
163:         context.subordinateCache,
164:       );
165:     }
166: 
167:     const updateData: Partial<typeof user.$inferInsert> = {};
168: 
169:     if (role !== undefined) {
170:       updateData.role = role;
171:     }
172: 
173:     if (banned === true) {
174:       updateData.banned = true;
175:       updateData.banReason = banReason ?? null;
176:       updateData.banExpires = banExpires ?? null;
177:     } else if (banned === false) {
178:       updateData.banned = false;
179:       updateData.banReason = null;
180:       updateData.banExpires = null;
181:     }
182: 
183:     if (permissions !== undefined) {
184:       updateData.permissions =
185:         role === "custom" || (role === undefined && permissions)
186:           ? permissions
187:           : null;
188:     }
189:     if (name !== undefined) updateData.name = name;
190:     if (email !== undefined) updateData.email = email;
191: 
192:     if (supervisorId !== undefined) {
193:       updateData.supervisorId = supervisorId;
194:     }
195: 
196:     if (Object.keys(updateData).length > 0) {
197:       await db.update(user).set(updateData).where(eq(user.id, userId));
198:     }
199: 
200:     if (banned === true) {
201:       await db.delete(session).where(eq(session.userId, userId));
202:     }
203: 
204:     if (role !== undefined || permissions !== undefined) {
205:       await db.delete(session).where(eq(session.userId, userId));
206:     }
207: 
208:     return { success: true };
209:   });
210: 
211: export const listUsers = protectedProcedure
212:   .meta({
213:     permission: {
214:       action: "manage-roles" as const,
215:       resource: "team_members" as const,
216:     },
217:   })
218:   .handler(async () => {
219:     const supervisor = alias(user, "supervisor");
220: 
221:     const users = await db
222:       .select({
223:         banExpires: user.banExpires,
224:         banned: user.banned,
225:         banReason: user.banReason,
226:         createdAt: user.createdAt,
227:         email: user.email,
228:         id: user.id,
229:         image: user.image,
230:         name: user.name,
231:         permissions: user.permissions,
232:         phoneNumber: user.phoneNumber,
233:         role: user.role,
234:         supervisorId: user.supervisorId,
235:         supervisorName: supervisor.name,
236:       })
237:       .from(user)
238:       .leftJoin(supervisor, eq(user.supervisorId, supervisor.id));
239: 
240:     return users;
241:   });
242: 
243: export const archiveUser = protectedProcedure
244:   .meta({
245:     permission: {
246:       action: "archive" as const,
247:       resource: "team_members" as const,
248:     },
249:   })
250:   .input(archiveUserSchema)
251:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
252:     await db
253:       .update(user)
254:       .set({ banned: true, banReason: "Archived by admin" })
255:       .where(eq(user.id, input.userId));
256:     return { success: true };
257:   });
258: 
259: export const listRoles = protectedProcedure
260:   .meta({
261:     permission: {
262:       action: "manage-roles" as const,
263:       resource: "team_members" as const,
264:     },
265:   })
266:   .handler(async () => {
267:     return listPredefinedRoles();
268:   });
269: 
270: export const resetPassword = protectedProcedure
271:   .meta({
272:     permission: {
273:       action: "manage-roles" as const,
274:       resource: "team_members" as const,
275:     },
276:   })
277:   .input(resetPasswordSchema)
278:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
279:     const hashedPassword = await hashPassword(input.newPassword);
280:     await db
281:       .update(account)
282:       .set({ password: hashedPassword })
283:       .where(
284:         and(
285:           eq(account.userId, input.userId),
286:           eq(account.providerId, "credential"),
287:         ),
288:       );
289:     return { success: true };
290:   });

(End of file - total 290 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: import { protectedProcedure } from "@/rpc/middleware";
2: import {
3:   addLinkSchema,
4:   archiveTaskSchema,
5:   createTaskSchema,
6:   getTaskByIdSchema,
7:   listEventsSchema,
8:   listTasksSchema,
9:   removeLinkSchema,
10:   taskStatsSchema,
11:   updateTaskSchema,
12: } from "@/rpc/schema/task";
13: import { listEvents } from "@/services/task-event-service";
14: import { addLink, removeLink } from "@/services/task-link-service";
15: import {
16:   archiveTask as archiveTaskService,
17:   createTask,
18:   getTask,
19:   getTaskStats,
20:   listTasks,
21:   updateTask,
22: } from "@/services/task-service";
23: 
24: export const taskCreate = protectedProcedure
25:   .input(createTaskSchema)
26:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
27:     const task = await createTask({
28:       ...input,
29:       createdBy: context.user.id,
30:       status: input.status ?? "todo",
31:     });
32:     return task;
33:   });
34: 
35: export const taskUpdate = protectedProcedure
36:   .input(updateTaskSchema)
37:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
38:     const { id, ...updates } = input;
39:     const userId = context.user.id;
40:     const task = await updateTask(id, updates, userId);
41:     return task;
42:   });
43: 
44: export const taskArchive = protectedProcedure
45:   .input(archiveTaskSchema)
46:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
47:     const userId = context.user.id;
48:     const task = await archiveTaskService(input.id, userId);
49:     return task;
50:   });
51: 
52: export const taskGetById = protectedProcedure
53:   .input(getTaskByIdSchema)
54:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
55:     const task = await getTask(input.id);
56:     if (!task) throw new Error("Task not found");
57:     return task;
58:   });
59: 
60: export const taskList = protectedProcedure
61:   .input(listTasksSchema)
62:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
63:     const result = await listTasks({
64:       cursor: input.cursor,
65:       filters: {
66:         archived: input.archived,
67:         assigneeId: input.assigneeId,
68:         deadlineFrom: input.deadlineFrom,
69:         deadlineTo: input.deadlineTo,
70:         entityId: input.entityId,
71:         entityType: input.entityType,
72:         search: input.search,
73:         status: input.status,
74:       },
75:       limit: input.limit,
76:       sortBy: input.sortBy,
77:       sortOrder: input.sortOrder,
78:     });
79:     return result;
80:   });
81: 
82: export const taskListEvents = protectedProcedure
83:   .input(listEventsSchema)
84:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
85:     return listEvents(input.taskId, input.cursor, input.limit ?? 50);
86:   });
87: 
88: export const taskAddLink = protectedProcedure
89:   .input(addLinkSchema)
90:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
91:     const link = await addLink(input.taskId, input.entityType, input.entityId);
92:     return link;
93:   });
94: 
95: export const taskRemoveLink = protectedProcedure
96:   .input(removeLinkSchema)
97:   .handler(async ({ input }) => {
98:     await removeLink(input.linkId);
99:     return { success: true };
100:   });
101: 
102: export const taskStats = protectedProcedure
103:   .input(taskStatsSchema)
104:   .handler(async () => {
105:     return getTaskStats();
106:   });

(End of file - total 106 lines)
</content>

1: import { relations } from "drizzle-orm";
2: import {
3:   boolean,
4:   foreignKey,

7:   pgTable,
8:   text,
9:   timestamp,
10: } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
11: 
12: import type { PermissionSet } from "@/lib/auth/access-control";
13: 
14: export const user = pgTable(
15:   "user",
16:   {
17:     banExpires: timestamp("ban_expires"),
18:     banned: boolean("banned").default(false),
19:     banReason: text("ban_reason"),
20:     createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow().notNull(),
21:     email: text("email").notNull().unique(),
22:     emailVerified: boolean("email_verified").default(false).notNull(),
23:     id: text("id").primaryKey(),
24:     image: text("image"),
25:     name: text("name").notNull(),
26:     permissions: jsonb("permissions").$type<PermissionSet>(),
27:     phoneNumber: text("phone_number").unique(),
28:     phoneNumberVerified: boolean("phone_number_verified"),
29:     role: text("role"),
30:     supervisorId: text("supervisor_id"),
31:     updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at")
32:       .defaultNow()
33:       .$onUpdate(() => /* @__PURE__ */ new Date())
34:       .notNull(),
35:   },
36:   (table) => [
37:     foreignKey({
38:       columns: [table.supervisorId],
39:       foreignColumns: [table.id],
40:       name: "user_supervisor_id_fkey",
41:     }).onDelete("set null"),
42:   ],
43: );
44: 
45: export const session = pgTable(
46:   "session",
47:   {
48:     createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow().notNull(),
49:     expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at").notNull(),
50:     id: text("id").primaryKey(),
51:     impersonatedBy: text("impersonated_by"),
52:     ipAddress: text("ip_address"),
53:     token: text("token").notNull().unique(),
54:     updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at")
55:       .$onUpdate(() => /* @__PURE__ */ new Date())
56:       .notNull(),
57:     userAgent: text("user_agent"),
58:     userId: text("user_id")
59:       .notNull()
60:       .references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
61:   },
62:   (table) => [index("session_userId_idx").on(table.userId)],
63: );
64: 
65: export const account = pgTable(
66:   "account",
67:   {
68:     accessToken: text("access_token"),
69:     accessTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("access_token_expires_at"),
70:     accountId: text("account_id").notNull(),
71:     createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow().notNull(),
72:     id: text("id").primaryKey(),
73:     idToken: text("id_token"),
74:     password: text("password"),
75:     providerId: text("provider_id").notNull(),
76:     refreshToken: text("refresh_token"),
77:     refreshTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("refresh_token_expires_at"),
78:     scope: text("scope"),
79:     updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at")
80:       .$onUpdate(() => /* @__PURE__ */ new Date())
81:       .notNull(),
82:     userId: text("user_id")
83:       .notNull()
84:       .references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
85:   },
86:   (table) => [index("account_userId_idx").on(table.userId)],
87: );
88: 
89: export const verification = pgTable(
90:   "verification",
91:   {
92:     createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow().notNull(),
93:     expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at").notNull(),
94:     id: text("id").primaryKey(),
95:     identifier: text("identifier").notNull(),
96:     updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at")
97:       .defaultNow()
98:       .$onUpdate(() => /* @__PURE__ */ new Date())
99:       .notNull(),
100:     value: text("value").notNull(),
101:   },
102:   (table) => [index("verification_identifier_idx").on(table.identifier)],
103: );
104: 
105: export const userRelations = relations(user, ({ many, one }) => ({
106:   accounts: many(account),
107:   sessions: many(session),
108:   subordinates: many(user, {
109:     relationName: "supervisor",
110:   }),
111:   supervisor: one(user, {
112:     fields: [user.supervisorId],
113:     references: [user.id],
114:     relationName: "supervisor",
115:   }),
116: }));
117: 
118: export const sessionRelations = relations(session, ({ one }) => ({
119:   user: one(user, {
120:     fields: [session.userId],
121:     references: [user.id],
122:   }),
123: }));
124: 
125: export const accountRelations = relations(account, ({ one }) => ({
126:   user: one(user, {
127:     fields: [account.userId],
128:     references: [user.id],
129:   }),
130: }));

(End of file - total 130 lines)
</content>

1: import { protectedProcedure } from "@/rpc/middleware";
2: import {
3:   archiveProspectSchema,
4:   createProspectSchema,
5:   getProspectByIdSchema,
6:   listProspectEventsSchema,
7:   listProspectsSchema,
8:   updateProspectSchema,
9: } from "@/rpc/schema/prospect";
10: import { listProspectEvents } from "@/services/prospect-event-service";
11: import {
12:   archiveProspect as archiveProspectService,
13:   createProspect,
14:   getProspect,
15:   listProspects,
16:   updateProspect,
17: } from "@/services/prospect-service";
18: 
19: export const prospectCreate = protectedProcedure
20:   .meta({ permission: { action: "create", resource: "prospects" } })
21:   .input(createProspectSchema)
22:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
23:     const prospect = await createProspect({
24:       ...input,
25:       createdBy: context.user.id,
26:     });
27:     return prospect;
28:   });
29: 
30: export const prospectUpdate = protectedProcedure
31:   .meta({ permission: { action: "update", resource: "prospects" } })
32:   .input(updateProspectSchema)
33:   .handler(async ({ input, context }) => {
34:     const { id, ...updates } = input;
35:     const userId = context.user.id;
36:     const prospect = await updateProspect(id, updates, userId);
37:     return prospect;
38:   });
39: 
40: export const prospectArchive = protectedProcedure

<path>/home/aditya/projects/alpauls-ats/src/services/supervisor-hierarchy-service.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { ORPCError } from "@orpc/server";
2: import { eq, sql } from "drizzle-orm";
3: 
4: import { db } from "@/lib/db/server";
5: import { user } from "@/schema";
6: 
7: export type SubordinateCache = Map<string, string[]>;
8: 
9: export async function getSubordinateIds(
10:   userId: string,
11:   cache?: SubordinateCache,
12: ): Promise<string[]> {
13:   const cached = cache?.get(userId);
14:   if (cached) return cached;
15: 
16:   const result = await db.execute(sql`
17:     WITH RECURSIVE subordinates AS (
18:       SELECT id FROM "user" WHERE supervisor_id = ${userId}
19:       UNION ALL
20:       SELECT u.id FROM "user" u
21:       INNER JOIN subordinates s ON u.supervisor_id = s.id
22:     )
23:     SELECT id FROM subordinates
24:   `);
25: 
26:   const ids = result.rows.map(
27:     (row: Record<string, unknown>) => row.id as string,
28:   );
29: 
30:   cache?.set(userId, ids);
31:   return ids;
32: }
33: 
34: export async function getDirectSubordinateIds(
35:   userId: string,
36: ): Promise<string[]> {
37:   const rows = await db
38:     .select({ id: user.id })
39:     .from(user)
40:     .where(eq(user.supervisorId, userId));
41: 
42:   return rows.map((row) => row.id);
43: }
44: 
45: export async function validateNoCircularSupervisor(
46:   userId: string,
47:   newSupervisorId: string,
48:   cache?: SubordinateCache,
49: ): Promise<void> {
50:   if (userId === newSupervisorId) {
51:     throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
52:       message: "A user cannot be their own supervisor",
53:     });
54:   }
55: 
56:   const subordinateIds = await getSubordinateIds(userId, cache);
57:   if (subordinateIds.includes(newSupervisorId)) {
58:     throw new ORPCError("BAD_REQUEST", {
59:       message: "Circular supervisor chain detected",
60:     });
61:   }
62: }

(End of file - total 62 lines)
</content>
